steps.title: Identify the river's transformation
steps.content: The passage pivots at the viaduct of Point du Jour, which “blocked the river” in Paris. Once past it, the Seine “suddenly spreading out as though it had regained space and liberty” becomes “the peaceful river” of the countryside. This shift reveals the narrator's characterization.

steps.title: Match the characterization
steps.content:
C captures the river's transformation exactly by describing it as “more confined and rough in Paris than they are in the countryside”—where “confined” reflects being blocked by the viaduct and “rough” captures the turbulence implied by the contrast with the peaceful countryside river.
  • A invokes water clarity (not discussed)
  • B reverses which section is peaceful
  • D wrongly elevates Paris
.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
